titleStmt.title: Letter from Samuel Alex Wray to the Henry Arthur Wynne, 19 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Samuel Alex Wray

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Samuel Alex Wray (b.1875), a solicitor, to Henry Arthur Wynne. In the letter, Wray states that he is the solicitor for Professor Valentine Steinberger (b.1853), a German professor in University College Galway who was arrested in Galway following the Easter Rising and deported to Wandsworth prison, and his wife Elizabeth Steinberger (b. 1858). Wray states that he wishes to consult Steinberger regarding his half of the rent payment, also requesting to be informed of Steinberger's whereabouts.Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1945) was the chief crown solicitor for Ireland during the Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5611/8624/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
SAMUEL A. WRAY,
SOLICITOR.
COMMISSIONER FOR OATHS
TELEPHONE N o 28
Attorney General
For your directions
newsletter June
20.V.16 COLERAINE. 19th May 1916. Dear Sir,
I act as solicitor and agent for Professor Steinberger of
Belmore Galway and his wife who are joint owners of some house
property in this town. My custom after completion of my collection
of the May and November rents has been to send a statement, and a
cheque for one half of amount payable, to each. Following upon the
Sinn Fein rebellion the Professor has been arrested and I would
be glad to hear from you if there would be any objection on the part
of the Crown to my remitting to him his half of the rents or taking
his instructions as to the disposal thereof and, if there is no such
objection, I would feel obliged if you would tell me where he is now
to be found. Yours faithfully, Samuel A. Wray
The Chief Crown Solicitor,
Dublin Castle,
Dublin.
Inform Mr Way that Professor
Steinberger is in the custody of
the military authorities & that he
should apply to them
MN 22.5.10
